reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the same core judo: stop competing on the incumbent's checklist, reframe the evaluation criteria, turn breadth into a liability, dominate a wedge metric, and shift who evaluates (developer vs procurement) - hitting all four key points. A is cleanly structured with no redundancy and explicitly calls out getting ahead of the RFP. B adds a useful extra move (concede loudly) and a strong closing diagnostic about naming the segment, but opens with a redundant sketch that previews the same points restated later. These minor differences roughly cancel out; neither has a factual or reasoning flaw and both are comparably actionable and complete.
